Title: IWS - Verify LLDP information on single Resense Move
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. A single IWS (Resense Move) device is connected to the Q-SYS Core network via Ethernet.
5. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
6. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open QPM in a web browser and navigate to the IWS device.

Title: 2. Open the "Basic" network page.
3. Review the LLDP neighbor information.
Preconditions: 1. The device's LLDP neighbor information is visible on the Basic network page in QPM.

Title: 2. The upstream switch/port is correctly identified.
3. The LLDP data matches the physical network topology.
IWS - Verify single Resense Move network topology in Q-SYS
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. A single IWS (Resense Move) device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
5. Device is connected directly to a Q-SYS Core or managed switch.
6. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open the network topology or status view in Q-SYS Designer.

Title: 2. Locate the IWS device in the topology.
3. Verify the displayed connection path.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device appears in the network topology.

Title: 2. The connection shows the correct upstream device (switch or Core).
3. The port number matches the physical connection.
IWS - Verify daisy-chained Resense Move devices are discovered
2. Create a new design.
3. Add two or more IWS components to the canvas.
4. Two or more IWS (Resense Move) devices are physically daisy-chained (Device A Ethernet out → Device B Ethernet in).
5. Inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
2. Review the discovered devices list.
Preconditions: 1. All daisy-chained IWS devices automatically appear in Configurator.

Title: 2. Each device shows a unique IP address and identifier.
3. All devices can be individually added to the design.
IWS - Verify LLDP topology shows daisy-chain order
2. Create a new design.
3. Add IWS components to the canvas for each daisy-chained device.
4. Two or more IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained, inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
5. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open the network topology or status view in Q-SYS Designer, or check the Basic network page in QPM for each device.

Title: 2. Locate the daisy-chained IWS devices in the topology.
3. Verify the displayed chain order.
Preconditions: 1. The topology correctly shows the daisy-chain order (Core/switch → Device A → Device B → ...).

Title: 2. Each device shows its upstream and downstream LLDP neighbor.
3. The first device in the chain shows the switch/Core as its upstream neighbor.
IWS - Verify sensor data flows through daisy-chained devices
2. Create a new design.
3. Add IWS components for each daisy-chained device.
4. Two or more IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained, inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
5. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click each IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, verify sensor data is present in the Sensor Output section for each device.
3. Introduce an environmental change near each device and observe updates.
Preconditions: 1. Each daisy-chained device independently reports its own sensor data.

Title: 2. Sensor values update in near real-time for all devices in the chain.
3. No data loss or cross-contamination between daisy-chained devices.
IWS - Verify daisy-chain resilience when middle device is disconnected
2. Create a new design.
3. Add IWS components for three daisy-chained devices (A → B → C).
4. All three devices are on the network, inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
5. Deploy the design to the Core and verify all devices are connected.
Preconditions: 1. Verify all three devices are reporting sensor data.

Title: 2. Disconnect Device B (middle device) from the chain by unplugging its Ethernet cable.
3. Observe the status of Device A and Device C.
4. Observe the status of Device B.
Preconditions: 1. Device A (upstream of the disconnection) continues to report sensor data normally.

Title: 2. Device C (downstream of the disconnection) loses connectivity and shows a fault/disconnected state.
3. Device B shows a fault/disconnected state.
4. No crash or unhandled error occurs in Q-SYS Designer.
IWS - Verify daisy-chain recovery when disconnected device is reconnected
2. Create a new design.
3. Three IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained (A → B → C).
4. Device B was previously disconnected, causing Device C to also lose connectivity.
Preconditions: 1. Reconnect Device B to the daisy-chain.

Title: 2. Wait for the devices to re-establish connection.
3. Observe the status of all three devices.
Preconditions: 1. Device B reconnects and resumes reporting sensor data.

Title: 2. Device C reconnects and resumes reporting sensor data.
3. All devices reconnect automatically without manual intervention.
4. The daisy-chain topology is restored correctly.
IWS - Verify LLDP updates when daisy-chain order changes
2. Create a new design.
3. Two IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained (A → B), inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Verify the current topology shows A → B order.

Title: 2. Physically swap the daisy-chain order to B → A.
3. Wait for LLDP to update the topology.
4. Review the network topology in Q-SYS Designer or QPM Basic network page.
Preconditions: 1. The topology updates to reflect the new B → A order.

Title: 2. Both devices remain functional.
3. Sensor data continues to flow for both devices.
IWS - Verify maximum daisy-chain depth
2. Create a new design.
3. The maximum supported number of daisy-chained IWS (Resense Move) devices are connected in a chain.
4. Inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core.

Title: 2. Verify all devices appear in Configurator.
3. Verify sensor data is flowing for all devices, including the last device in the chain.
Preconditions: 1. All devices in the chain are discovered and connected.

Title: 2. Sensor data is available for every device, including the last in the chain.
3. No degradation of data update frequency for downstream devices.
IWS - Verify LED control works on all daisy-chained devices
2. Create a new design.
3. Two or more IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained, inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
4. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click each IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the "LED Configuration" tab for each device.
3. Set a different LED color on each device (e.g., Device A = red, Device B = green).
4. Observe the physical devices.
Preconditions: 1. Each device's LED ring changes to the configured color independently.

Title: 2. LED commands reach all devices in the chain, including downstream devices.
3. No LED command interference between daisy-chained devices.
IWS - Verify firmware update on daisy-chained devices
2. Create a new design.
3. Two or more IWS (Resense Move) devices are daisy-chained, inventory item names match device names in Configurator.
4. The devices are running an older firmware version than what the Core provides.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core, triggering the automatic firmware update on all daisy-chained devices.

Title: 2. Monitor the update progress for each device.
Preconditions: 1. Firmware update completes successfully on all daisy-chained devices.

Title: 2. All devices reconnect after the update.
3. The daisy-chain topology is preserved after the update.
4. No device in the chain is bricked or left in a fault state.
